sourceDesc.p: Derived from CDSL MW, PWG, PWK, GRA, FRI, and BEN source records for interoperability analysis.

projectDesc.p: tei-archival-profile-v0.1; TEI is used as the archival/textual model. Raw CDSL records are preserved as escaped source-entry quotes, while selected lexical structures are promoted into TEI entry, form, sense, citation, and relation elements.

revisionDesc.change: Included in the full machine-reviewed TEI profile.

sense.note: No stable machine definition could be extracted; source record is preserved in cit/quote.

listBibl.head: Machine-extracted citation index; source quotes preserve the archival text.

cit.quote:
<L>3404<pc>1-0253<k1>antika<k2>antika/
{#antika/#}¦
<div n="1"> 1) <lex>adj.</lex>
<div n="2"> a) {%mit%} oder {%an Etwas das Ende erreichend, bis wohin reichend%} (räumlich oder zeitlich), am Ende eines <ab>comp.</ab>: {#keSAntika#} {%bis an die Haare reichend%} (Stab), {#nAsAntika#}
<ls>M. 2,46.</ls> {#zawriMSadAbdikaM caryaM gurO trEvedikaM vratam . tadarDikaM pAdikaM vA grahaRAntikameva vA#} ({%oder bis zur Erlernung dauernd%})
<ls n="M.">3,1.</ls> <ls>YĀJÑ. 1,36.</ls> Zum Ueberfluss wird noch {#A#} dem Endpunkt vorgesetzt: {#anyo'nyasyAvyaBicAro BavedAmaraRAntikaH#} {%eine-gegenseitige Treue erstrecke sich bis zum Tode%}
<ls>M. 9,101.</ls> {#daRqaH prARAntikaH#} {%eine Strafe, die das Ende des Lebens nach sich zieht, Todesstrafe%}
<ls n="M.">8,379.</ls> <ls>R. 4,28,32.</ls> = {#prARAnta#}
<ls>M. 8,359.</ls> In {#prARAntikakaraRaM vEram#}
<ls>PAÑCAT. 157,1.</ls> ist {#karaRa#} ganz überflüssig und störend.
<div n="2">— b) {%nahe%}
<ls>AK. 3,2,17.</ls> <ls n="AK. 3,">4,32,14.</ls> (<ls>COL. 3,4,28,14.</ls>) <ls>MED. k. 43.</ls> <ls>PAT.</ls> zu <ls>P. 8,2,84</ls> (<ab>vgl.</ab> <ab>u.</ab> {#anavasTita#} 1.). Mit dem <ab>gen.</ab> oder <ab>abl.</ab>
<ls>P. 2,3,34.</ls> <ab>superl.</ab> {#antikatama#} {%überaus nahe%}
<ls>AK. 3,2,18.</ls>
<ls>H. 1452.</ls>
<ls>NIR. 5,28.</ls> Der <ab>entspr.</ab> <ab>comp.</ab> {#nedIyaMs#}, <ab>superl.</ab> {#nedizWa#}
<ls>P. 5,3,63.</ls>
<ls>VOP. 7,56.</ls>
<div n="1">— 2) <lex>f.</lex> {#˚kA#} .
<div n="2"> a) {%eine ältere Schwester%} (im Drama)
<ls>AK. 1,1,7,15.</ls>
<ls>TRIK. 3,3,3.</ls>
<ls>H. 335.</ls>
<ls>an. 3,5.</ls>
<ls>MED. k. 43.</ls> <ab>Vgl.</ab> 2. {#anti, attikA, arttikA#} .
<div n="2">— b) {%Opfen%}
<ls>AK. 2,9,29.</ls>
<ls>TRIK. 3,3,3.</ls>
<ls>H. 1018.</ls>
<ls>an. 3,4.</ls>
<ls>MED. k. 43.</ls> <ab>Vgl.</ab> {#antI#} und {#andikA#} .
<div n="2">— c) <ab>N.</ab> einer Pflanze
<ls>H. an. 3,4</ls> ({#sAtalAKyOzaDe#}).
<ls>MED. k. 43</ls> ({#SAtalOzaDO#}), heut zu Tage {#cAmArakazA#}
<ls>ŚKDR.</ls> <ab>Vgl.</ab> {#carmakazA#} .
<ls>WILS.</ls> : {%Echites (Alstonia) scholaris.%}
<div n="1">— 3) <lex>n.</lex> {%Nähe%}
<ls>AK. 3,4,185.</ls>
<ls>TRIK. 3,3,3.</ls>
<ls>H. 1450.</ls>
<ls>an. 3,4.</ls> mit dem <ab>gen.</ab> oder <ab>abl.</ab>
<ls>P. 2,3,34.</ls> {#na tyajanti mamAntikam#} {%sie lassen nicht von meiner Seite%}
<ls>HIT. I,40.</ls> Am Anfange eines <ab>comp.</ab> vor einem Verbalnomen: {%in der Nähe, nahebei%}: {#antikasTa#}
<ls>ŚĀK. 161.</ls> {#˚sTita#}
<ls>VID. 73.</ls> {#˚nyasta#}
<ls>RAGH. 2,24.</ls> {#karRAntikacara#} {%um die Ohren schwirrend%} (eine Biene)
<ls>ŚĀK. 22.</ls> Davon
<div n="2"> a) <ab>acc.</ab> {#antikam#} {%in die Nähe, herbei%}: {#AnAyayadrAjakanyA brAhmaRAkftimantikam#}
<ls>KATHĀS. 16,19.</ls> <ls n="KATHĀS.">24,168.</ls> mit dem <ab>gen.</ab> oder <ab>abl.</ab>
<ls>P. 2,3,34.</ls> <ls n="P. 2,3,">35.</ls> {#antikaM grAmasya#} oder {#grAmAt#}
<ab>Sch.</ab> {%zu — hin, auf — zu, vor — hin%}: {#yadi^ mf\tyora^nti\kaM nI^ta e\va#}
<ls>ṚV. 10,161,2.</ls> {#kakzIvato 'ntikamAsasAda, dIrGatamaso 'ntikamAgatya#}
<ls>SĀY.</ls> zu <ls>ṚV. 1,125,1.</ls> {#dUrasTasyEtya cAntikam#}
<ls>M. 2,197.</ls> {#mamAntikaM prAptaH#}
<ls>INDR. 5,31.</ls> {#pravizwe piturantikam#}
<ls>R. 2,17,16.</ls> {#enAmAnayeha mamAntikam#}
<ls>N. 13,24.</ls>
<ls>HIḌ. 2,12.</ls> {#antikaM mAtuH — yayO#}
<ls>VID. 155.</ls> mit dem <ab>acc.</ab>: {#niryAtu ca BavAnyazwuM yajYAyatanamantikam#} (der <ab>acc.</ab> könnte auch mit {#niryAtu#} in Verbindung gesetzt werden; <ab>vgl.</ab> indessen weiter unten {#antikAt#})
<ls>R. 1,12,35.</ls> <ab>compon.</ab>: {#mfgAntikaM calitaH#}
<ls>HIT. 43,19.</ls> <ab>Vgl.</ab> {#janAntikam#} . {%nahe von%}: {#tasyeva hyetadantikaM tizWati#}
<ls>ŚAT. BR. 1,4,5,3.</ls> {#rAmAdrudrasya yo dUraM pApAdduHKasya so 'ntikam#}
<ls>VOP. 5,22.</ls> <ab>vgl.</ab> {#samantikam#} .
<div n="2">— b) <ab>instr.</ab> {#antikena#} {%nahe bei%}: {#antikena grAmasya#}
<ls>P. 2,3,35</ls>, <ab>Sch.</ab>
{#SmaSAnasyAntikena saH . AgacCannaSfRodetAM tanmaDyAdudgatAM giram#}
<ls>KATHĀS. 25,129.</ls>
<div n="2">— c) <ab>abl.</ab> {#antikAt#}
<ls>P. 2,3,35.</ls> <is>gaṇa</is> {#svarAdi#} (<ab>indecl.</ab>). {%aus der Nähe%}: {#a\nti\kAdi^va paSyati#}
<ls>AV. 4,16,1.</ls> <ls n="AV.">12,2,38.</ls> <ls n="AV. 12,">50,52.</ls> {#apaSyam — taddEtyapuramantikAt#}
<ls>ARJ. 6,7.</ls> {#ya imaM maDvadaM veda AtmAnaM jIvamantikAt#}
<ls>KAṬHOP. 4,5.</ls> mit einem <ab>part.</ab> <ab>praet.</ab> <ab>pass.</ab> componirt
<ls>P. 2,1,39.</ls> <ls n="P.">6,3,2.</ls> {#antikAdAgataH#} <ab>Sch.</ab> Accent eines solchen <ab>comp.</ab>
<ls>SIDDH. K.</ls> zu <ls>P. 6,2,49.</ls>
[Page1-0254]
{%in der Nähe, dicht bei%}: {#tasmA etamantikAdgoptAramakurvan#}
<ls>ŚAT. BR. 6,7,1,5.</ls> {#rajaHkaREH — spfSadBirgAtramantikAt#}
<ls>RAGH. 1,85.</ls> mit dem <ab>gen.</ab>: {#antikAdgrAmasya#}
<ls>P. 2,3,35</ls>, <ab>Sch.</ab>
mit dem <ab>acc.</ab>: {#ime ca puruzA divyA yAntyasya raTamantikAt#}
<ls>R. 3,9,11.</ls> {%in die Nähe von%}, mit dem <ab>acc.</ab>: {#uvAha mAM tataH SIGraM hiraRyapuramantikAt#} (der <ab>acc.</ab> könnte auch mit {#uvAha#} verbunden werden)
<ls>ARJ. 10,18</ls>; <ab>vgl.</ab> {#antikam#} oben. {%von%} (kaufen, hören), mit dem <ab>gen.</ab>: {#kriRIyAdyastvapatyArTaM mAtApitroryamantikAt#}
<ls>M. 9,174.</ls> {#nEva pravittiM SfRumastayoH kasyacidantikAt#}
<ls>R. 4,49,6.</ls>
<div n="2">— d) <ab>loc.</ab> {#antike#} {%in der Nähe, dicht an%}: {#ja\hi Satru^manti\ke dU^ra\ke ca^#}
<ls>ṚV. 9,78,5.</ls>
<ls>ŚAT. BR. 1,6,1,21.</ls>
<ls>ĪŚOP. 5.</ls>
<ls>BHAG. 13,15.</ls>
<ls>AMAR. 15.</ls> {#yaM haMsaM samupADAvadantike#}
<ls>N. 1,25.</ls> {#enamupAveSayadantike#}
<ls>INDR. 2,20.</ls> mit dem <ab>gen.</ab>: {#damayantyAstadAntike nipetuH#}
<ls>N. 1,22.</ls> am Ende eines <ab>comp.</ab>: {#gfhAntike#}
<ls>M. 11,188.</ls>
<ls>YĀJÑ. 3,297.</ls> <ls n="YĀJÑ.">1,148.</ls> {#tasTurfziputrAntike#}
<ls>R. 1,9,13.</ls> {#viprAntike — upanikzipet#}
<ls>M. 3,224.</ls> <ls n="M. 3,">218.</ls> {%in Gegenwart von%}, mit dem <ab>gen.</ab>: {#antike striyAH#}
<ls>M. 2,202.</ls> {#vadizyAmyanftam — kaTamatra tavAntike#}
<ls>BRAHMA-P. in LA. 58,1.</ls> — In der ersten Bedeutung von {#anta#}, in der <ab>Bed.</ab> {%nahe, Nähe%} von 1. {#anti#}; <ab>vgl.</ab> {#antima#} .
<LEND>

<L>5444<pc>1-064-b<k1>antika<k2>antika {#antika#}¦ <div n="1">— 1〉 *<lex>Adj.</lex> {%nahe%}. <div n="1">— 2〉 *<lex>f.</lex> {#A#} <div n="2">— a〉 im Drama {%ältere Schwester%}. <div n="2">— b〉 {%eine <ab>best.</ab> Pflanze%}. <div n="2">— c〉 Ofen <ls>GAL.</ls> <div n="1">— 3〉 <lex>n.</lex> {%Nähe%}. {#antika/m#} {%in die Nähe, herbei; zu — hin, auf — zu, vor — hin;%} mit <ab>Gen.</ab> und *<ab>Abl.</ab> oder am Ende eines <ab>Comp.</ab> (so auch bei den folgenden Casus). {#antikena#} {%nahe bei%}. {#antikA/t#} {%aus der Nähe; in der (die) Nähe bei%} <ls n="Chr.">54,7</ls>. <ls n="Chr.">78,25</ls>. von (<ab>z. B.</ab> kaufen) <ls n="Chr.">199,5</ls>. {#antike/#} {%in der Nähe, dicht an; in Gegenwart von%}. {#antika˚#} {%in der Nähe%}. <LEND>

<L>586<pc>0066<k1>antika<k2>antika/ {@antiká,@}¦ <ab>a.</ab>, nahe [von ánti]; im <ls>RV</ls> nur <ab>n.</ab>, {%Nähe,%} -ám, {%in die Nähe,%} -é, {%in der Nähe%}. <div n="TS">-ám mṛtyós {987,2}. <div n="TS">-é {790,5} <ab>Gegens.</ab> dūraké. <LEND>
<L>367<pc>023<k1>antika<k2>antika antika n. <div n="1"/>1 <lang n="czech">blízkost</lang> <div n="1"/>2 <lang n="russian">близость</lang> <div n="1"/>3 <lang n="english">vicinity</lang> <LEND>

<L>544<pc>0028-b<k1>antika<k2>antika {#antika#}¦ {%anti + ka%} (from the <ab>ved.</ab> anti = <lang n="greek">ἀντί</lang>, <ab>cf.</ab> <lang n="greek">ἄντα, ἄντην</lang>; <ab>Lat.</ab> ante, <ab>Goth.</ab> and, e. g. in and-hafjan, to answer; <ab>N.H.G.</ab> ant-, ent-, e. g. in ant-worten, ent-gegnen), <ab>n.</ab> Vicinity, Rājat, 5, 57. -- <ab>abl.</ab> {%kāt.%} Near, with <ab>acc.</ab> <ls>Rām.</ls> 3, 9, 11. From, with <ab>gen.</ab> <ls>Man.</ls> 9, 174. -- <ab>loc.</ab> {%ke.%} Close to, <ls>Nal.</ls> 1, 25. In presence of, <ls>Man.</ls> 2, 202. {@ -- <ab>Comp.</ab>@} {%Keśa-,%} <ab>i. e.</ab> {%keśānta%} {%+ ika,%} reaching to the hair, <ls>Man.</ls> 2, 46. {%Grahaṇa-,%} <ab>i. e.</ab> {%grahaṇānta + ika,%} lasting till the comprehension, <ls>Man.</ls> 3, 1. {%ja-%} {%nāntikam,%} <ab>i. e.</ab> {%jana-antika + m,%} <ab>adv.</ab> whispering, speaking aside, <ls>Śāk.</ls> 13, 12. <LEND>
